checks.finding: Every inline decision carries an explicit rationale. design.md Design Decisions section states 'Rationale:' text for both new (non-OQ-carried) decisions (completeness-check insertion point before sanitization; native .ps1 Get-ChildItem -Recurse re-implementation vs. shell-out, citing epic-159-pillar-d precedent). API/Contract Plan justifies the ERE-escape reuse ('mirrors emit-run-record.sh:123's own sed escape... defense in depth if the grammar is ever loosened'), the .ps1 anchor pattern reuse ('mirroring emit-run-record.ps1:139's own already-landed anchor'), and the CI step shape ('matching the combined-suite convention... NOT a (bash)/(pwsh) pair, since the suite already drives both target scripts internally'). Constraint Compliance table gives a design-response rationale for every listed row. No unjustified 'we will use X' statement found.

design.md's '## Open Questions' section contains zero entries ('None blocking. All investigation.md OQ-1..OQ-6 are resolved above with design decisions...'). All six carried-forward OQs (OQ-1..OQ-6) are instead resolved with cited rationale in the 'Design Decisions (resolving open questions)' section, each naming an owner-equivalent verification method (e.g. OQ-1: 'verified directly against guard_invariants.py:4,18'). No entry requiring owner/Blocks-Implementation/Resolution-Path fields exists to evaluate; check is vacuously satisfied.

investigation.md is present (requirements.md line 19: 'Investigation: specs/quality-loop-fixes/investigation.md (INV-001..INV-028, OQ-1..OQ-6)'; not in my allowed_input_manifest, so not independently opened, per fail-closed scope). design.md's Assumptions section carries forward requirements.md's five assumptions verbatim, each citing a specific INV-xxx or direct-verification basis: OQ-1's protected-file finding (INV-020..022), the run-all.ps1 combined-suite exclusion convention (confirmed by direct enumeration against three named existing suites), the run-all.sh-present/test.yml-absent registration state (INV-005), the identity-ledger tail snapshot (INV-024, sequence 319), and emit-run-record.sh:125's anchor form (INV-007). All five are grounded per criterion 1 (INV-xxx citation) and additionally carry a WFI-013 re-verification instruction rather than being treated as permanently true.

Primary (TYPE-D) path applies: design.md has a '## Constraint Compliance' section (lines 544-557) with 9 rows. Cross-checked against requirements.md constraint language (grep for must/must not/Must-Preserve): baseline preservation (REQ-006/BL-001..BL-012) is addressed; the path-traversal 'must not' constraint on Stream 3's completeness check (requirements.md Edge Cases, line 493-495; Security Boundaries B1) is addressed via the 'declared-outputs path containment (Security Boundaries B1)' row; the tamper-detection 'must still fail' constraint (Security Boundaries B2) is addressed via the 'identity-chain tamper non-regression (Security Boundaries B2)' row; the two protected-file carve-outs are addressed; bash-3.2/.ps1-exit resilience constraints (INV-026) are addressed; version-bump-only and doc-following constraints (REQ-007) are addressed. No requirements.md constraint found unaddressed. Layer specs (ux/frontend/infra/security) are consistent with design.md and requirements.md -- no silent override found (all state 'N/A -- no change' or restate design.md's own boundaries verbatim).

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. Grep for numeric-threshold/SLA keywords (response time, throughput, concurrency, latency, 'within N ms', 'maximum N') across requirements.md returned no matches; design.md's own Test Strategy item 4 and infra-spec.md's Runtime Budget section both explicitly state 'No runtime-budget assertion is added... no live network call, no subprocess-loop-driving beyond what existing suites already do,' consistent with the absence of any stated performance requirement.

design.md's '## Deployment / CI Plan' section (lines 526-543) states the deployment target (no runtime deployment; existing 3-OS CI matrix via .github/workflows/test.yml, staged/applied by the human maintainer as pre-merge commits on the feature PR branch), the exact CI pipeline change (Stream 1's one new bash-only step, YAML given verbatim in API/Contract Plan and mirrored in infra-spec.md CI/CD Sequence), and rollback ('a reviewed revert of its own commits; Stream 1's rollback additionally requires a second human-copy application reverting both ship/SKILL.md's prose and .github/workflows/test.yml's registration line'). Data Plan states no schema changes (see MIGRATION-PLANNED), so migration-execution-order is not applicable and its absence is not a defect. Feature-flag strategy is not addressed, but this feature is a direct-merge bugfix set with no incremental rollout claimed anywhere in requirements.md or design.md, so the conditional clause ('if the feature is being rolled out incrementally') does not apply.

design.md Data Plan states: 'Data Entities: none new... Existing Data Affected: none of the 4 streams writes a new field into any existing report or ledger schema... Migration Strategy: none. No schema changes; the identity ledger's own append-only, sequence-chained shape... is untouched by Stream 4 -- the fix changes how bytes are READ from jq -r output, never what is WRITTEN to the ledger.' This is functionally equivalent to the check's 'No data changes' PASS condition -- no column/table change is declared anywhere in design.md or requirements.md's Data Plan-equivalent content.

design.md's '## External Integrations' section states: 'None. All 4 streams operate entirely on repository-local files... no new network call, external API, or credential is introduced by any stream.' No external system, third-party service, or cross-service API call is referenced anywhere else in design.md, requirements.md, or infra-spec.md/security-spec.md in passing without detail -- local CLI tool dependencies (jq, python3, git, bash, pwsh) are documented in infra-spec.md's Runtime Dependencies table with purpose and absence-behavior, but these are not external systems/services subject to this check.

Every design.md Component-table entry maps 1:1 to a requirements.md Goal: check-quality-gate-cycle-limit.{sh,ps1}/ship-SKILL.md/test.yml -> REQ-001; emit-run-record.{sh,ps1} -> REQ-002; prepare-panelist-input.{sh,ps1} -> REQ-003; cross-model-verify/SKILL.md -> REQ-004; validate-review-context-set.sh -> REQ-005; bash-3.2/.ps1-exit constraints -> REQ-006; CHANGELOG entries -> REQ-007. No component, data model, or API endpoint appears in design.md that lacks a corresponding REQ-NNN/Goal in requirements.md (Cross-Layer Dependencies table confirms the same mapping explicitly). Conversely, all 7 Goals (REQ-001..REQ-007) and all 4 target-user stories are covered by a named design component -- no omitted capability found.

High-risk surfaces present: B1 path-containment/traversal risk (Stream 3), B2 identity-chain tamper-detection risk (Stream 4), B3 protected-file bypass risk (Stream 1), and Stream 1's externally-visible CLI-contract break. Each names a concrete validation path: B1 -> TEST-014..017 plus TEST-032's dedicated adversarial '../'-traversal/absolute-path negative case (acceptance-tests.md) plus security-spec.md's Security Tests row citing the same IDs; B2 -> TEST-026 re-running BL-010's tampered-ledger cases; B3 -> TEST-006/TEST-007 plus 'sdd-hook-guard.py PreToolUse enforcement' named as defense-in-depth (security-spec.md STRIDE row); Stream-1 CLI break -> TEST-001..007. No high-risk surface was found lacking a named test ID, CI job, or review artifact.
